title: workbench-ynab config.json
description: User configuration for the workbench-ynab plugin. Lives OUTSIDE the repo at $HOME/.claude/plugins/data/workbench-ynab-claude-workbench/config.json so it survives plugin updates. Read by plugin SKILLS and commands via bin/config.sh — never by the vendored MCP launcher. All tax logic is data-driven: any user's situation is one config instance, never a schema constant.

schema_version.description: Config schema version, for forward migration. This schema describes version 2 (the multi-budget shape, issue #84). Version-1 files (singular `budget`) keep working: bin/config.sh synthesizes a single-entry `budgets` array at read time and leaves the file untouched — re-run /workbench-ynab:setup to upgrade the file itself.

budgets.description: The YNAB budgets this plugin operates on. Replaces the schema-v1 singular `budget` object: every skill resolves its target budget set from this array instead of assuming one budget. Per-budget overrides are resolved BY SKILLS as each skill is built — this config layer only carries the data.

budget_id.description: YNAB budget id (UUID). Preferred over budget_name when known — ids are rename-proof. At least one of budget_id / budget_name is required.

budget_name.description: Human-readable budget name as shown in YNAB, used to resolve the id at runtime. At least one of budget_id / budget_name is required.

label.description: Human display string for this entry, unique across the array. Used in report headings and as the lookup key for per-budget config reads (bin/config.sh `_cfg_budget_field`) and for `default_budget`.

role.description: Role/tag for this budget, e.g. `personal`, `business`, `archive`. Free-form; skills may group or filter by it.

business_category_group.description: Optional per-budget override: name of the YNAB category group holding this budget's business expense categories.

tax_profile_path.description: Optional per-budget override: path to a tax-profile file linked to this budget, so the tax tracker can attribute Schedule C activity to the correct budget. Supports ~ and environment-variable expansion at use time.

monitoring_enabled.description: Optional per-budget override: whether the monitoring poll covers this budget. Defaults to true when absent.

write_back_enabled.description: Optional per-budget override: whether the write-back path may target this budget. Defaults to true when absent; the mandatory human approval gate applies regardless.

default_budget.description: Optional. The `label` of the budgets entry to use when a caller needs a single budget and does not specify one — the fallback for single-budget users. When absent, the first budgets entry is the default (bin/config.sh `_cfg_default_budget`).

timezone.description: REQUIRED. The single source of truth for ALL date math (issue #31): the review's 7-day lookback window, carryover boundary, month start/end, quarterly windows, and tax-year resolution all compute against this timezone, never the host clock. An IANA tz identifier — e.g. `America/Phoenix` — mirroring bujo's convention. `/workbench-ynab:setup` resolves your system's zone at setup time and writes it here as an explicit identifier; it is NEVER stored as "system local", so scheduled and interactive runs are deterministic. The loader (`bin/config.sh` `_cfg_timezone`) validates it against the tz database at load time and FAILS CLOSED — a descriptive error, never a silent fall-through to the host clock — on a missing or invalid value. This `pattern` is a shape check only; the loader's zoneinfo lookup is the authoritative validity gate — it requires the name to resolve to a compiled TZif zone file (not merely any file of that name), so zoneinfo housekeeping artifacts (leapseconds, +VERSION, tzdata.zi) and the UTC-equivalent pseudo-zones (Factory, posixrules) are rejected.

business.description: Optional side-business configuration. Replaces the prototype's hardcoded business name, business checking account, and expense categories. Omit entirely if the user has no business.

name.description: Business display name, used in report headings and Schedule C context.

accounts.description: Names of the YNAB accounts that belong to the business (e.g. a business checking account).

category_group.description: Name of the YNAB category group that holds business expense categories.

expense_categories.description: Names of the YNAB categories treated as deductible business expenses.

tax_profile.description: Data-driven tax profile. Holds only generic tax parameters — filing status, public IRS amounts/rates, due dates, and which schedules apply. No personal income figures, account ids, or names. The canonical, deeply-validated tax sub-schema is owned by the Sprint 2 tax engine (issues #20/#21/#22); this envelope defines the top-level shape the loader and setup rely on.

filing_status.description: IRS filing status. Drives standard-deduction and bracket selection downstream.

standard_deduction.description: Standard deduction amount for the filing status and tax year. A PUBLIC IRS figure (not a personal amount); the user verifies it for the current year.

medical_agi_threshold_pct.description: Fraction of AGI above which unreimbursed medical expenses become deductible on Schedule A (e.g. 0.075 for 7.5%). Public IRS rule.

se_tax_rate.description: Self-employment tax rate applied to net SE earnings (e.g. 0.153 for 15.3%). Public IRS rule.

quarterly_due_dates.description: Estimated-tax quarterly due dates as MM-DD strings (year-agnostic; the active year is resolved at runtime).

schedules.description: Which IRS schedules apply to this profile. Required and non-empty.

mapping_rules.description: Payee/category to tax-line mapping rules, expressed as DATA (not code). Each rule matches transactions and assigns a schedule/tax-line. The full matching engine and heuristics are owned by the Sprint 2 classifier (issue #23); this envelope defines the array shape the loader exposes.

match.description: Match criteria. Any combination of the keys below; all present keys must match.

payee_contains.description: Case-insensitive substring matched against the transaction payee.

category.description: Exact YNAB category name to match.

category_group.description: Exact YNAB category group name to match.

schedule.description: Schedule this rule maps matched transactions onto.

tax_line.description: Human-readable tax line / category the matched transactions roll up into.

persona.description: The financial-review persona. The name is a CONFIG FIELD, not a constant: the default voice is supplied by the persona skill (issue #36), and the user may rename it here.

name.description: Display name of the review persona. When omitted, the persona skill applies its shipped default name. Validated at config-load time (issue #28): at most 64 characters, no control characters — bin/persona.sh validate-name enforces this loudly during setup. Not marked required here to match that default behaviour (AC 7: a missing or empty name falls back silently to the shipped default), mirroring report.output_dir below.

voice_overrides.description: Optional per-user voice/tone notes layered on top of the shipped voice (free text, at most 500 characters — longer values are truncated with a logged warning). Null or omitted to use the shipped voice. STYLE ONLY (issue #28): rendered into the model context as delimited DATA by `bin/persona.sh voice`; it can never authorize, expand, or alter a YNAB write.

output_dir.description: Directory where generated HTML reports are written. Supports ~ and environment-variable expansion at use time. OPTIONAL — when absent or empty the writer (bin/report-writer.sh) applies the shipped default ~/Documents/Claude/Reports, so the loader and tests deliberately exercise the field-absent path. Not marked required here to match that default behaviour.

template_path.description: Path to the frozen HTML report template. When null, the plugin's bundled template under assets/ is used. The bundled template is frozen in Sprint 3 (issue #42).

schedules.description: Scheduled-task cadences for the plugin's background tasks. Read by the setup step (or a /workbench-ynab:setup re-run) to deploy and sync tasks via the scheduled-tasks MCP — cadence is config-driven, never hardcoded in a skill or task deployment. Omit the whole block to accept the defaults.

review.description: The unified review (Sprint 3). ONE scheduled task (ynab-review) whose cron fires /workbench-ynab:ynab-review; the read-only orchestrator routes every tier (weekly/monthly/quarterly-tax/annual). NOT four per-tier tasks — a single cadence covers them all.

cron.description: Cron expression for the unified review. Defaults to Monday 07:00 ("0 7 * * 1") — the proven weekly cadence — when the block or field is absent. The orchestrator routes tiers, so this one cadence covers weekly/monthly/quarterly-tax/annual.

enabled.description: Whether the ynab-review scheduled task is deployed. Defaults to true. Set false and re-run setup to remove or disable the task; the ynab-monitor task is unaffected.

monitor.description: Proactive between-run monitoring poll (M6). Runs more frequently than the weekly review and is a distinct scheduled task (ynab-monitor).

cron.description: Cron expression for the monitoring poll. Defaults to daily at 08:00 ("0 8 * * *") when the block or field is absent.

enabled.description: Whether the ynab-monitor scheduled task is deployed. Defaults to true. Set false and re-run setup to remove or disable the task; the weekly-review (ynab-review) task is unaffected.

alerts.description: Alert rules for proactive between-run monitoring (M6, docs/alerts-config.md). Thresholds are user-tunable data read exclusively by the monitoring skill — never injected into the YNAB MCP launcher environment. Dollar amounts are entered in whole dollars and converted to YNAB milliunits (× 1000) at load time. Omit the whole block to accept the defaults — monitoring works with no configuration (zero-config).

enabled.description: Master switch for alert dispatch. Defaults to true. Set false to silence all alerts; the monitor pass still advances its state snapshot.

large_transaction_amount.description: A single transaction at or above this amount (WHOLE DOLLARS) is alert-worthy. Converted to milliunits (× 1000) at load time. Defaults to 500.

unusual_multiplier.description: A transaction this many times the category's typical spend counts as unusual. Defaults to 3.

budget_overrun_pct.description: A category spent at or beyond this percentage of its budgeted amount is overrun. Defaults to 100 (at/over budget).

bill_due_lookahead_days.description: How many days ahead an upcoming scheduled bill is worth flagging. Defaults to 3.

overdrawn.description: Whether a negative account balance is alert-worthy. Defaults to true.

channel.description: Delivery-channel switch (docs/alerts-config.md). 'macos-notification' (default) fires a best-effort macOS desktop notification; 'log-only' stops at the audit log. Every dispatch appends to the alert log regardless of channel.

apply.description: Optional configuration for the /workbench-ynab:ynab-apply approval command (M4-5). Omit entirely to use the shipped defaults.

proposal_path.description: Override for the directory the approval command reads pending change-set proposals from. When omitted, defaults to the proposals/ subdir of the plugin data dir. The weekly review (M4-10) writes proposals here; coordinate the exact filename when M4-10 lands.

classification.description: Optional confidence-band thresholds for the classification → human-review routing policy (issue #19, docs/confidence-contract.md). Confidence governs proposal composition only — whether an op is pre-filled in the apply proposal; the human approval gate is mandatory and independent of confidence. Omit to accept the conservative shipped defaults (high 0.85, medium 0.60). Read by lib/tax/confidence.mjs loadThresholds(); invalid or contradictory values fall back to the defaults.

highThreshold.description: confidence ≥ highThreshold → band 'high': the op is eligible to be pre-filled in the apply proposal (still subject to the human approval gate).

mediumThreshold.description: mediumThreshold ≤ confidence < highThreshold → band 'medium': rendered as a 'review suggested' item in the report, never pre-filled into a proposal. Must be strictly less than highThreshold — a contradictory pair falls back to the defaults.
